summary.efficiency_delta: with_skill uses +411 output-char tokens on average

observations: Iteration-2 assertions became discriminating: with_skill achieved 100% mean pass rate while without_skill dropped to 93% mean due to one failing eval.
The failing baseline case was eval-2 (audit-log-export-service), missing explicit tests section and polish phase.
Path-hygiene assertion passed for both configurations in this sample; quality lift came from structural completeness checks.
Timing/token telemetry from subagent notifications was unavailable; time is 0 and token values proxy output size.
recommendations: Keep structural validation language in SKILL.md; it appears to improve phase/test completeness robustness.
Refine path-hygiene assertion further to detect escaped markdown in non-task sections if needed.
In a future run, capture real telemetry to validate runtime/token cost tradeoffs.
